---
title: "reference_scaled_dot_product_attention"
generated: true
description: "Reference scaled-dot-product attention (oracle)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.nn.attention`](../nitrix-nn-attention.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.nn.attention._reference`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:reference_scaled_dot_product_attention -->
```python
reference_scaled_dot_product_attention(q: Float[Array, '... h s d'], k: Float[Array, '... h t d'], v: Float[Array, '... h t d_v'], *, bias: Optional[Float[Array, '... h s t']] = None, mask: Optional[Bool[Array, '... h s t']] = None, scale: Optional[float] = None, causal: bool = False, qk_norm: bool = False) -> Float[Array, '... h s d_v']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:reference_scaled_dot_product_attention -->
Reference scaled-dot-product attention (oracle).

Computes
$\operatorname{softmax}(\mathrm{scale} \cdot q\,k^{\top} + b)\,v$
over the key axis `t`, where the softmax logits are masked (set to
$-\infty$) at excluded entries before normalisation and the
additive bias $b$ is optional.  Leading dimensions `...` (batch)
and the head axis `h` are handled by `einsum` broadcasting, and
`bias` / `mask` broadcast against `(..., h, s, t)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:reference_scaled_dot_product_attention -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `q` | `Float` | `...` × `h` × `s` × `d` | — | Query / key / value. `q` and `k` share head dim `d`; `k` and `v` share key length `t`. `s` may differ from `t` (cross-attention). |
| `k` | `Float` | `...` × `h` × `t` × `d` | — | Query / key / value. `q` and `k` share head dim `d`; `k` and `v` share key length `t`. `s` may differ from `t` (cross-attention). |
| `v` | `Float` | `...` × `h` × `t` × `d_v` | — | Query / key / value. `q` and `k` share head dim `d`; `k` and `v` share key length `t`. `s` may differ from `t` (cross-attention). |
| `bias` | `Optional[Float[Array, '... h s t']]` | — | `None` | Optional additive logit bias (e.g. a Swin relative-position table), broadcast to `(..., h, s, t)`. |
| `mask` | `Optional[Bool[Array, '... h s t']]` | — | `None` | Optional boolean keep-mask (`True` keeps an entry); broadcast to `(..., h, s, t)`. Combined with `causal` by logical-and. |
| `scale` | `Optional[float]` | — | `None` | Softmax temperature; defaults to `1 / sqrt(d)`. |
| `causal` | `bool` | — | `False` | If `True`, query `i` attends only to keys `j <= i` (indices aligned at 0 -- the standard self-attention convention). |
| `qk_norm` | `bool` | — | `False` | If `True`, RMS-normalise `q` and `k` over the head dim before the dot (QK-norm; logit-growth control at depth/scale). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...` × `h` × `s` × `d_v`. Attention output of shape `(..., h, s, d_v)` in the common result type of `q`, `k` and `v`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:reference_scaled_dot_product_attention -->
::: {.callout-note}
All compute runs in at least float32: a float16 or bfloat16 input is
upcast to float32 for QK-norm, scores, softmax and value aggregation, and
cast back at the end, so the oracle is platform-stable (no bfloat16
tensor-core multiply variance); float64 inputs stay float64.  A fully
masked query row yields `nan` (a degenerate case; callers should keep at
least one key per query).
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:reference_scaled_dot_product_attention -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `h` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `s` | flattened spatial sample positions |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `t` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d_v` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
